findings.one_line: Reproducing the feasible-vs-oracle decomposition gives half-width `cv_{1-alpha}^{sel} hat sigma_n + hat B_n(M) + hat g_{gen,n} + hat g_{shift,n} + hat g_{rem,n}`; the proof only controls `hat g_{shift,n}` and the second-order remainder, so the displayed order `O_P((n h_n)^(-1/2)+M h_n^2+rho_n h_n)` drops the first-order generated-score envelope `hat g_{gen,n}` unless an extra rate assumption makes it negligible.

From the declared dependencies you get left-continuity and sign preservation on `[0,M_*(P)]` plus zero-inclusion for every `M>M_*(P)`, but no right-limit step at `M_*(P)`; the conclusion `L_*(P,M_*(P)) = 0` or `U_*(P,M_*(P)) = 0` therefore needs an added dependency or argument establishing continuity/nonempty-budget control just to the right of the frontier.

Once `H_*(P,M)` is assumed nonempty, the reproduced compactness-and-attainment argument uses the range bound on `Delta`, the definition of `H_*(P,M)`, and well-defined `tau_*(P)`, but it never consumes the extra `P_atom(M)` restrictions such as `ass:local-two-sided-atom-support` or `ass:score-cef-extension`; the theorem is stated on a smaller class than the discharged route requires.
